clusters.title: Deadly Philippines earthquake unleashes small tsunami
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera, BBC World and others

items.title: Deadly Philippines earthquake unleashes small tsunami
items.standfirst: A deadly earthquake rocked the southern Philippines, killing dozens of people and sending small tsunami waves toward at least three nations.
items.content: A deadly earthquake rocked the southern Philippines, killing dozens of people and sending small tsunami waves toward at least three nations.

items.title: Southern Philippines hit by 7.8-magnitude earthquake
items.standfirst: Offshore quake rocks Mindanao, killing at least 35 and injuring more than 200.
items.content: Offshore quake rocks Mindanao, killing at least 35 and injuring more than 200.

items.title: At least 35 dead after major earthquake strikes southern Philippines
items.standfirst: The magnitude-7.8 quake triggered small tsunami waves in the Philippines, Indonesia and Japan.
items.content: The magnitude-7.8 quake triggered small tsunami waves in the Philippines, Indonesia and Japan.

items.title: Powerful earthquake in southern Philippines leaves at least 35 dead
items.standfirst: People told not to enter damaged buildings for fear of aftershocks from magnitude-7.8 quakeAt least 35 people have died after a magnitude-7.8 earthquake shook part of the southern Philippines early on Monday, collapsing buildings and triggering tsunami alerts.The quake hit early in the morning about 20km (12.4 miles) off the coast of Sarangani province, with tremors felt strongly across Mindanao and 420km away in the city of Manado on the Indonesian island of Sulawesi. Continue reading...
items.content: People told not to enter damaged buildings for fear of aftershocks from magnitude-7.8 quakeAt least 35 people have died after a magnitude-7.8 earthquake shook part of the southern Philippines early on Monday, collapsing buildings and triggering tsunami alerts.The quake hit early in the morning about 20km (12.4 miles) off the coast of Sarangani province, with tremors felt strongly across Mindanao and 420km away in the city of Manado on the Indonesian island of Sulawesi. Continue reading...

items.title: Tsunami alert and 35 dead after earthquake hits the Philippines
items.standfirst: At least 35 people have died after a magnitude-7.8 earthquake struck the island of Mindanao in the Philippines.
items.content: At least 35 people have died after a magnitude-7.8 earthquake struck the island of Mindanao in the Philippines.

clusters.title: Israel and Iran step back from further strikes after renewed clashes
clusters.neutral_headline: Has Trump lost control of the Iran war
bullet_summary: US President Donald Trump told them to stop fighting, as Israel reported 30 missiles from Israel and Israel struck back
Reported by France24, CBS News, BBC World and others

items.title: Israel and Iran step back from further strikes after renewed clashes
items.standfirst: Israel and Iran appeared to pull back from further military action on Monday after exchanging fire for the first time since a US-brokered ceasefire two months ago. Both sides warned they would retaliate if attacked again, while regional powers and the Trump administration pushed to prevent the conflict from escalating into a wider war.
items.content: Israel and Iran appeared to pull back from further military action on Monday after exchanging fire for the first time since a US-brokered ceasefire two months ago. Both sides warned they would retaliate if attacked again, while regional powers and the Trump administration pushed to prevent the conflict from escalating into a wider war.

items.title: Iran and Israel say attacks halted after Trump tells both to "stop 'shooting'"
items.standfirst: Iran and Israel declare a halt to fighting as President Trump says both are seeking an "immediate ceasefire" after a major escalation in the 101-day war.
items.content: Iran and Israel declare a halt to fighting as President Trump says both are seeking an "immediate ceasefire" after a major escalation in the 101-day war.

items.title: Has Trump lost control of the Iran war?
items.standfirst: For the first time since the start of a precarious ceasefire two months ago, Israel and Iran have traded missile strikes.
items.content: For the first time since the start of a precarious ceasefire two months ago, Israel and Iran have traded missile strikes.

items.title: Iran war: who is fighting and why?
items.standfirst: Arch-enemies Israel and Iran have returned to active confrontation while Donald Trump tries to present himself as mediatorIsrael and Iran have returned to active war for the first time since a ceasefire was agreed two months ago in an exchange of rocket fire that threatened efforts to end the conflict.Donald Trump, who started the war in February alongside Israel but has since attempted to present himself as a mediator, told the two sides to stop shooting and said “final negotiations” on peace were proceeding. By late afternoon on Monday, the attacks had stopped. Continue reading...
items.content: Arch-enemies Israel and Iran have returned to active confrontation while Donald Trump tries to present himself as mediatorIsrael and Iran have returned to active war for the first time since a ceasefire was agreed two months ago in an exchange of rocket fire that threatened efforts to end the conflict.Donald Trump, who started the war in February alongside Israel but has since attempted to present himself as a mediator, told the two sides to stop shooting and said “final negotiations” on peace were proceeding. By late afternoon on Monday, the attacks had stopped. Continue reading...

items.title: Israel shuts down following war flare-up with Iran
items.standfirst: Iran and Israel say they've now halted attacks, after their first exchange of fire since a ceasefire in April. US President Donald Trump told them to stop fighting, as Israel reported 30 missiles from Israel and Israel struck back. Among ordinary Israelis, it stoked of a return to full-blown war.
items.content: Iran and Israel say they've now halted attacks, after their first exchange of fire since a ceasefire in April. US President Donald Trump told them to stop fighting, as Israel reported 30 missiles from Israel and Israel struck back. Among ordinary Israelis, it stoked of a return to full-blown war.

items.title: U.S. bars entry of FIFA World Cup referee from Somalia
items.standfirst: Federal immigration authorities barred a Somali soccer referee who was slated to officiate the FIFA World Cup from entering the U.S. over the weekend, citing "vetting concerns."
items.content: Federal immigration authorities barred a Somali soccer referee who was slated to officiate the FIFA World Cup from entering the U.S. over the weekend, citing "vetting concerns."

items.title: Somali referee Artan barred from entering USA
items.standfirst: Omar Artan, who was set to be the first Somali to referee at the World Cup finals, is dropped from the list of officials after he was denied entry to the United States.
items.content: Omar Artan, who was set to be the first Somali to referee at the World Cup finals, is dropped from the list of officials after he was denied entry to the United States.

items.title: US confirms it denied entry to Somali referee set to take part in World Cup
items.standfirst: Award-winning referee Omar Artan was 'determined to be inadmissible due to vetting concerns' after arriving in Miami.
items.content: Award-winning referee Omar Artan was 'determined to be inadmissible due to vetting concerns' after arriving in Miami.

clusters.title: Trump storms out of NBC interview after denying his own “No War” promises
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump storms out of NBC interview after denying his own “No War” promises
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, CBS News, NBC World

items.title: Trump storms out of NBC interview after denying his own “No War” promises
items.standfirst: Donald Trump stormed off from his interview for NBC’s “Meet the Press," after he was fact-checked on his false claims about election fraud by reporter Kristen Welker. She also asked him about the Iran war, his controversial "anti-weaponization" fund and the cost of living, though Trump made a serious of false and exaggerated responses. He also denied having made campaign "promises" about starting any wars before walking out entirely.
items.content: Donald Trump stormed off from his interview for NBC’s “Meet the Press," after he was fact-checked on his false claims about election fraud by reporter Kristen Welker. She also asked him about the Iran war, his controversial "anti-weaponization" fund and the cost of living, though Trump made a serious of false and exaggerated responses. He also denied having made campaign "promises" about starting any wars before walking out entirely.

items.title: Trump walks out of interview with NBC
items.standfirst: President Trump abruptly ended an interview with NBC News when repeatedly challenged on his claims of election fraud
items.content: President Trump abruptly ended an interview with NBC News when repeatedly challenged on his claims of election fraud

items.title: Read the transcript: President Donald Trump interviewed by NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’ moderator Kristen Welker
items.standfirst: Trump spoke about the war with Iran, the “anti-weaponization” fund, the economy, recent elections in California and more in June 2026 interview.
items.content: Trump spoke about the war with Iran, the “anti-weaponization” fund, the economy, recent elections in California and more in June 2026 interview.

items.title: Fact-checking Trump’s interview with NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’
items.standfirst: Digging into some of the president’s claims about the Iran war, the Jan. 6 riots, California’s primary elections and more.
items.content: Digging into some of the president’s claims about the Iran war, the Jan. 6 riots, California’s primary elections and more.

clusters.title: China’s Xi Jinping and North Korea’s Kim Jong Un meet in Pyongyang
clusters.neutral_headline: China’s Xi Jinping and North Korea’s Kim Jong Un meet in Pyongyang
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, NBC World

items.title: China’s Xi Jinping and North Korea’s Kim Jong Un meet in Pyongyang
items.standfirst: Chinese President Xi Jinping and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un met on Monday at a rare summit in Pyongyang.
items.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un met on Monday at a rare summit in Pyongyang.

items.title: ‘A special sense of closeness’: China’s Xi vows stronger ties with North Korea in rare visit
items.standfirst: Chinese President Xi Jinping on Monday said that China world not swerve from its commitment ​to safeguarding common interests with North Korea or waver in its support for Kim Jong Un during a rare summit between the two leaders in Pyongyang. The summit comes at a time when China's economy, strengthened by growing trade and military ties to Russia, could boost ⁠North Korea's confidence in talks.
items.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping on Monday said that China world not swerve from its commitment ​to safeguarding common interests with North Korea or waver in its support for Kim Jong Un during a rare summit between the two leaders in Pyongyang. The summit comes at a time when China's economy, strengthened by growing trade and military ties to Russia, could boost ⁠North Korea's confidence in talks.

items.title: Kim Jong Un hosts Xi Jinping from a position of rare strength
items.standfirst: Kim Jong Un offered China’s president a grand welcome Monday.
items.content: Kim Jong Un offered China’s president a grand welcome Monday.

clusters.title: Russian interference in Armenia: A nation tested by disinformation
clusters.neutral_headline: Armenia's pro-West government wins election despite Russian pressure
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Russian interference in Armenia: A nation tested by disinformation
items.standfirst: Under the leadership of Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan, Armenia is moving to distance itself from Russia and forge closer ties with the European Union. Despite pro-Russian disinformation campaigns targeting the government, the pro-European candidate won the parliamentary elections held on June 7.
items.content: Under the leadership of Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan, Armenia is moving to distance itself from Russia and forge closer ties with the European Union. Despite pro-Russian disinformation campaigns targeting the government, the pro-European candidate won the parliamentary elections held on June 7.

items.title: Armenia's pro-West government wins election despite Russian pressure
items.standfirst: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract Party secures nearly 50% of the vote, comfortably beating the other contenders.
items.content: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract Party secures nearly 50% of the vote, comfortably beating the other contenders.

items.title: Armenia’s pro-Europe party wins election and cements shift away from Russia
items.standfirst: Result strengthens PM Nikol Pashinyan’s drive for deeper integration with Europe despite warnings from MoscowArmenia’s ruling pro-Europe party has won parliamentary elections, confirming the country’s pivot towards Europe and away from its traditional ally, Russia.Final results in the small South Caucasus country showed the prime minister Nikol Pashinyan’s Civil Contract party securing a slim majority, while the Strong Armenia alliance, led by the Russian-Armenian billionaire Samvel Karapetyan, won 25% of the seats in parliament. Continue reading...
items.content: Result strengthens PM Nikol Pashinyan’s drive for deeper integration with Europe despite warnings from MoscowArmenia’s ruling pro-Europe party has won parliamentary elections, confirming the country’s pivot towards Europe and away from its traditional ally, Russia.Final results in the small South Caucasus country showed the prime minister Nikol Pashinyan’s Civil Contract party securing a slim majority, while the Strong Armenia alliance, led by the Russian-Armenian billionaire Samvel Karapetyan, won 25% of the seats in parliament. Continue reading...

clusters.title: ‘Senior lieutenant’ in Kinahan criminal cartel jailed for 24 years by Dublin court

items.title: ‘Senior lieutenant’ in Kinahan criminal cartel jailed for 24 years by Dublin court
items.standfirst: Sean McGovern pleaded guilty to two charges relating to a deadly feud after being extradited from the UAEA leader of the notorious Kinahan criminal cartel has been sentenced to 24 years in prison at a Dublin court.Sean McGovern, 40, who has been described as a senior lieutenant in the group, pleaded guilty to two charges of directing the activities of a criminal organisation relating to a deadly feud between the Kinahan and Hutch criminal gangs. items.content: A senior figure in a major organised crime gang has been sentenced to 24 years in prison at a court in Dublin.
